meta.schema_note: One object per day. `wellness` and `activity` are raw-ish pulled metrics; `score`+`components` from DAY_SCORE.md; `entry` is the prose; `prediction` is the HM time; reviewed=false means routine-drafted and not yet human-amended.
meta.scoring: probability-change model v5 (see DAY_SCORE.md): score=ΔP(1:20), score_committed=ΔP(1:25) — INDEPENDENT trajectories that diverge (neither always higher); 60=neutral; equal-100 ceilings; magnitude discipline; green-recovery floor; breakthrough/illness/cross-train channels; decoupling by run type; Pacific tz
meta.timezone: athlete US Pacific (UTC-8/-7); Intervals UTC but start_date_local is Pacific; evening runs cross midnight UTC
meta.prob_model: absolute P(1:20)/P(1:25) per DAY_SCORE.md — transparent model estimate, not measured; watch the trend
meta.model_version: v7: two-invocation; probability = current-fitness raced-TODAY readout (~0% now, climbs with fitness); no forward-dated plans; Pacific tz

days.planned: Easy long run 65 min @ 5:45/km flat (~11.3 km), optional 4x20s strides

components.label: Long run — longest since Mar 22; base is exactly what 1:20 lacks
vs 1:25: helps, but smaller ΔP (1:25 needs less base, already closer)
Ran hotter than prescribed (5:24 vs 5:45, drift to 167)
Decoupling ~6% mostly the negative split (progression) — weighted lightly

days.entry: Longest run since March 22 — and here the two lines separate in a way that's actually informative. Against the 1:20 dream it's a clear raiser (70): aerobic base is precisely what that goal is short on, so banking it moves the hard-goal odds up. Against the realistic 1:25 (66) it helps less — you're already closer to on-track for 1:25, so the same run buys a smaller probability gain. That 1:20-above-1:25 split is the signature of base work: it disproportionately helps the goal starving for base. Ran hotter than prescribed (5:24 vs 5:45, HR→167), which costs a little; I'm weighting the ~6% decoupling lightly since this was a progression (rising HR is mostly the negative split, not base deficit). Recovery green, cadence steady 172-174.
days.note_next: Mon Jul 6: OFF or ≤25 min true jog (load-77 bill). Tue intervals conditional on quiet Monday.
days.prob_note: current-fitness readout (raced-today odds). ~0% now — projected half ~1:37 vs 1:20/1:25 targets; the gap is fitness, not build risk. Rises as fitness improves.
days.plan_rationale: Base-phase long run: build the aerobic base the 1:20 lacks, at easy effort. (Prescribed the night before under the old model; going forward, plans are set the morning-of.)

components.label: Short 5 km on a fresh, well-recovered body — banks some aerobic time
Recovery green (sleep excellent, HRV 89 ≥ baseline, RHR 46 floor)
Ran hotter than a recovery slot ideally wants — didn't help as much as easy would have
Green-recovery floor: a rested short run has NOT lowered the odds
vs 1:25 slightly higher: tighter 1:20 is marginally less forgiving

days.entry: Short 5 km on a well-recovered body (sleep excellent, HRV 89 above baseline, RHR 46 at floor). ~Neutral against both goals — a rested, willing short run has not lowered the 1:20 odds (58 vs 1:20, 61 vs 1:25). It was run hotter than a pure recovery slot ideally wants, so it 'didn't help as much as truly easy would have' — a neutral, not a negative; the green-recovery floor prevents the model from ever scoring a fresh short-run day into the 40s (an earlier version wrongly did). The two lines sit close today with 1:25 a hair higher, since the tighter 1:20 is marginally less forgiving of the extra intensity. Logged correctly to July 6 Pacific.
days.note_next: Tue Jul 7: FULLY OFF — Monday-quiet condition not met, so intervals move to Wed Jul 8. Tue homework: first weighted calf session. Protect sleep.
days.prob_note: current-fitness readout (raced-today odds). ~0% now — projected half ~1:37 vs 1:20/1:25 targets; the gap is fitness, not build risk. Rises as fitness improves.
days.plan_rationale: Prescribed OFF/true-jog to absorb the prior long run; recovery was green but the day did not call for intensity.
